title: Pyrus tengyuehensis
intro: Pyrus tengyuehensis, also known as Tengyue pear, is a plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its unique fruit and medicinal properties. The health benefits of Pyrus tengyuehensis include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may contribute to the treatment of various diseases such as diabetes, cardiovascular disorders, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Pyrus tengyuehensis is often prepared into decoctions, teas, and capsules, as well as being used to make extracts and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and sore throat.
taxonomy: Pyrus tengyuehensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a member of the genus Pyrus, which includes various species of pear trees. The species is native to China and has been identified as a new species by botanists. Within the Rosaceae family, Pyrus tengyuehensis is also classified as a part of the subfamily Amygdaloideae. The exact classification and relationships within the Rosaceae family are still being studied and refined by botanists.
